originalAlarm.description: `curr` could be null (null value originating from line 65) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_RECURSIVE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Known bug for 2631486f1bf.c is dfree call at L33 (RECURSIVE=T). This alarm: curr = malloc() at L65 can return NULL, then curr->val at L66 is a null dereference. New bug. The null dereference exists in ALL configs — malloc can return NULL regardless of RECURSIVE or SORTFILES. The PC {RECURSIVE=T, SORTFILES=F} is unnecessarily narrow. Over-constrained.
